key_insight: Optimizing exchange-correlation functionals only for surface binding energies can degrade surface reaction barrier predictions unless key physical exact conditions are enforced.
engineering_relevance: For catalytic materials modeling, this highlights that DFT functional tuning must balance adsorption and transition-state accuracy to avoid misleading reactivity trends.
method: Evaluate exchange-correlation approximations on surface binding energies.
Compare performance on surface reaction barriers for solids and surfaces.
Use physically exact conditions as regularizers during functional optimization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a comparative evaluation showing that surface-binding-energy-optimized functionals can worsen barrier descriptions when exact conditions are omitted.
watch_item: A functional that fits adsorption energetics well may still be unreliable for reaction kinetics, so validation must include both energies and barriers.
paper_title: Physical exact conditions as regularizers for exchange-correlation in solids and surface chemistry
